attack_paths.description: User input flows into raw SQL or NoSQL queries without parameterisation, allowing authentication bypass or data exfiltration.
Authentication can be forged or bypassed because JWT signing keys and admin credentials are committed in source and password hashes use unsalted MD5.
Authorisation checks are absent server-side or enforced only in the Angular client, allowing any authenticated user to reach admin routes or modify other users' data.
Confidential files, access logs, JWT signing keys, and the unencrypted SQLite database are reachable via unauthenticated HTTP endpoints or readable from the container filesystem.
User-supplied content is executed server-side via an unsafe VM sandbox escape in the B2B order processor and via a template-injection sink in the user-profile route.
Angular components bypass default template escaping by calling bypassSecurityTrustHtml, and search results render user-controlled input into the DOM without sanitisation.

State-changing endpoints accept requests without CSRF tokens or SameSite cookie constraints; combined with wildcard CORS, cross-origin requests from attacker-controlled pages reach authenticated API routes.
